channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Weekly Canadian business roundup: economy slows, bank profits rise, and Tim Hortons adjusts foreign hiring strategy
item.description: Canada’s economy showed signs of stagnation in the first quarter, contracting at an annualised rate of 0.1 per cent, according to Statistics Canada. This follows a 1 per cent annualised decline in the...

item.title: DA wins Evaton ward in Emfuleni by-election, signalling shift in traditional ANC support
item.description: Democratic Alliance (DA) leader and Cape Town mayor Geordin Hill-Lewis has described the party’s by-election victory in Ward 28 in Evaton, Gauteng, as a significant turning point in South African poli...

item.title: Vivid Sydney cancels drone performances following Cockle Bay drone malfunction and safety investigation
item.description: Vivid Sydney has cancelled all remaining drone light shows after a serious technical failure during a performance at Cockle Bay resulted in dozens of drones falling into Sydney Harbour. The incident o...

item.title: 15-year-old girl dies at Formby beach during record-breaking May heatwave
item.description: A 15-year-old girl, Chiedza Nyanjowa, tragically died after encountering difficulties in the water at Formby beach, Merseyside, on Monday, becoming the 13th death linked to the record-breaking May hea...

item.title: Vanilla Ice Stands by Performance at America's 250th Anniversary Celebration as Other Artists Withdraw
item.description: Rapper Vanilla Ice has defended his decision to perform at the Great American State Fair's Freedom 250 concerts, part of the United States' 250th anniversary celebration. The event, taking place on th...

item.title: Aprilia dominates Mugello qualifying as Bezzecchi secures pole and Martin breaks MotoGP speed record
item.description: Aprilia enjoyed a historic qualifying session at the Italian Grand Prix at Mugello on Saturday, with Marco Bezzecchi claiming pole position and team-mate Jorge Martin setting a new MotoGP top-speed re...

item.title: Lagos Court Remands Blogger Over Defamation Allegations Against Tony Elumelu
item.description: For Lagos, a Federal High Court don put one blogger, Nwanorue John Surpruchi, for jail over alleged defamatory and cyberstalking posts wey concern Tony Elumelu, the UBA CEO. The Inspector General of P...

item.title: United States expands coordinated Ebola response funding and support across DRC, Uganda, and regional partners
item.description: The United States Department of State, working closely with the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), has stepped up its coordinated response to the ongoing Ebola outbreak affecting t...

item.title: Former DOJ official rejects Bondi’s effort to shift responsibility for handling of Epstein files
item.description: A former U.S. Department of Justice official has pushed back against former Attorney General Pam Bondi’s effort to deflect responsibility for the Justice Department’s handling of materials related to ...

item.title: Canadiens reflect on defeat to Hurricanes and focus on future development
item.description: The Montreal Canadiens are turning their attention to long-term development after a disappointing loss to the Carolina Hurricanes, a result that underscored both the team’s current growing pains and i...

item.title: Stakeholders call for reforms to rebuild trust between police and communities
item.description: Stakeholders from different sectors have held a meeting focused on finding solutions to restore public trust in the police force, as concern continues to grow over weakening relations between law enfo...

item.title: Cayetano rejects claims of rushed Senate rules changes amid warnings of destabilization
item.description: Senator Alan Peter Cayetano has denied accusations that he and his allies are rushing or “railroading” proposed changes to Senate rules, insisting that the process remains within proper parliamentary ...

item.title: Kenya Strengthens Ebola Preparedness at JKIA Despite No Confirmed Cases
item.description: Kenya is stepping up its Ebola preparedness at Jomo Kenyatta International Airport (JKIA) amid rising cases in the Democratic Republic of Congo, where over 1,000 infections and more than 200 deaths ha...

item.title: Sikho Nqothole earns mandatory position for IBF junior bantamweight world title fight after defeating Charlie Edwards
item.description: South African boxer Sikho “Sequence” Nqothole moved a significant step closer to a world title opportunity after defeating England’s Charlie Edwards in an IBF junior bantamweight elimination bout at Y...

item.title: Liverpool axe Arne Slot after disappointing title defence as club seeks tactical evolution
item.description: Liverpool have dismissed head coach Arne Slot following a disappointing defence of their Premier League title. The club, which secured its second league crown under Slot just a year prior, endured a s...

item.title: WHO Director-General Travels to Eastern DR Congo to Assess Ebola Response
item.description: On Saturday, WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus arrived in Bunia, the capital of Ituri province in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo, which has been severely hit by a new Ebola outbrea...

item.title: Divorce Records Raise Questions About Financial Hardship Claims in Belle Burden's Memoir
item.description: A Fox News report examines claims made by author and Vanderbilt heiress Belle Burden in her memoir, 'Strangers: A Memoir of Marriage,' in which she described fears of financial ruin following her divo...

item.title: Several Artists Withdraw from Trump’s Freedom 250 State Fair Over Political Concerns
item.description: Multiple well-known musical acts, including Milli Vanilli, The Commodores, Martina McBride, Brett Michaels, and Young MC, have announced their withdrawal from Donald Trump’s Freedom 250 celebration at...

item.title: Columnist Examines the Tight Los Angeles Mayoral Race and Critiques Spencer Pratt’s Campaign
item.description: In this opinion column, Steve Lopez revisits his criticism of mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt and reflects on the increasingly competitive race for mayor of Los Angeles. Lopez notes that Pratt did not...

item.title: Four More Men Successfully Rescued from Partially Flooded Laos Cave
item.description: In Laos, rescue teams have successfully freed four additional men who were trapped in a partially submerged cave, intensifying hopes for a full rescue operation. The trapped men, along with others alr...
